channel.title: INtech
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: One startup’s pitch to provide more reliable AI answers: crowdsource the chatbots
item.description: CollectivIQ looks to give users more accurate answers to their AI queries by showing them responses that pull information from ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude...

item.title: 16 Best Air Purifiers (2026): Coway, AirDoctor, IQAir
item.description: Protect your home against dust, pets, allergies, and more with air purifiers tested firsthand by WIRED.

item.title: Marshall Kilburn III Review: A Classic Rock Bluetooth Speaker
item.description: Prepping for warmer weather? Marshall's midsize portable Bluetooth speaker sounds as good as it looks.

item.title: I Ranked 30 Energy Drinks, From Celsius to Ghost (2025)
item.description: The future is here, and it is jacked up on B vitamins, red dye, and taurine. These are the best energy drinks to get from tired to wired.

item.title: The Best Linux Laptops (I Install Linux on Every Laptop I Test) (2026)
item.description: Whether you want a beast of a machine with dedicated graphics or a light, easy-to-upgrade PC, we have the perfect rig for you.

item.title: Why AI startups are selling the same equity at two different prices
item.description: Some AI founders are using a novel valuation mechanism to manufacture unicorn status.

item.title: Some People See Aliens While on DMT. Researchers Want to Find Out What They Can Teach Us
item.description: A new psychedelic retreat calling itself a “SETI for the mind” aims to establish two-way communication with the nonhuman entities people encounter whi...

item.title: Who needs data centers in space when they can float offshore?
item.description: Offshore wind developer Aikido will deploy a small data center beneath a floating offshore wind turbine later this year.

item.title: Eight Sleep raises $50M at $1.5B valuation
item.description: Eight Sleep said it was free-cash-flow positive in 2025, and plans to use the new funding for new products, global expansions, and clinical validation...

item.title: A suite of government hacking tools targeting iPhones is now being used by cybercriminals
item.description: Security researchers say exploits used by governments to hack into iPhones have been found to be used by cybercriminals. They warned of an emerging ma...
